message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Background</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    is an inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine approved by the World Health Organization. Previous studies reported increased levels of neutralizing antibodies and specific T cells two- and four-weeks after two doses of CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    , but the levels of neutralizing antibodies are reduced at six to eight months after two doses. Here we report the effect of a booster dose of CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    on the anti-SARS-CoV-2 immune response generated against variants of concern (VOC) Delta and Omicron in adults participating in a phase 3 clinical trial in Chile.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Volunteers immunized with two doses of CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    in a four-week interval received a booster dose of the same vaccine between twenty-four and thirty weeks after the 2nd dose. Four weeks after the booster dose, neutralizing antibodies and T cell responses were measured. Neutralization capacities and T cell activation against VOC Delta and Omicron were detected at four weeks after the booster dose.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Findings</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    We observed a significant increase in neutralizing antibodies at four weeks after the booster dose. We also observed an increase in CD4
                    <jats:sup>+</jats:sup>
                    T cells numbers over time, reaching a peak at four weeks after the booster dose. Furthermore, neutralizing antibodies and SARS-CoV-2 specific T cells induced by the booster showed activity against VOC Delta and Omicron.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Interpretation</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    Our results show that a booster dose of CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    increases the anti-SARS-CoV-2 humoral and cellular immune responses in adults. Immunity induced by a booster dose of CoronaVac
                    <jats:sup>®</jats:sup>
                    is active against VOC, suggesting an effective protection.
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: A booster dose of an inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine increases neutralizing antibodies and T cells that recognize Delta and Omicron variants of concern

reference.article-title: WHO approval of Chinese CoronaVac COVID vaccine will be crucial to curbing pandemic [Internet]
